So tonight I jumped on to play a few games.
1st round my team mates are completely silent until the moment I need sound to 1v2 clutch and hear them grabbing defuse. I lose due to 12vies screeching irellevant information over each other.
I tell them they are stupid and I lost that because of them and will be muting.  I disable voice and text. They proceed to take turns team kill me and throw the rest of the game.

Next game I am playing against them at which point it becomes abundantly obvious they are rage cheating because they know it is me. I also find out by the sound of a teen team mate who is still figuring out how to deal with hormones screeching in my ear that the disable audio setting doesnt work and also prevents you from individually disabling people.

After watching the replay to see the atrocious game skill and blatant walling from the players who had been team killing me I queue again, this time I am pleased to see none of the people from the previous games. 
My pleasure turns back into unsurprised disappointment when I realize that two coppers on the enemy team are clearly cheating and again I can hear my team mates screeching without being able to mute them. 
After losing again and again watching the replay of what I can only assume is two high teenagers with walls/esp and soft aim, I am out of time and my vening siege sesh is over.

This is just one example but this is a great representation of the bugs cheaters and toxicity you can expect to encounter when you start this game.

probably the single worst game ever developed by human hands, used to have a bit of fun on it back in the day, but returning now with a fully formed frontal lobe i can see clearly how disgusting it is, the movement, the gun play, the abilities, the maps, the controls, the teammates, and the UI, are all some of the worst aspects of any game i have ever seen in the industry, not to mention how it has consistently become worse and worse as time went on, guns and attachments nerfed over and over and over and over again to the point where the gun play is an absolute disgrace, it takes about 3-5 business days to aim down sight, and when you do, you can expect recoil that kicks harder than a genetically modified work horse, the movement is slow and sluggish and loud and clunky, the maps are genuinely awful and confusing and ugly, im going to stop now as im getting bored of even THINKING about Tom Clancy's Rainbow Six Siege. and lets be real even the name is dreadful like what even is that

This game could actually be so peak, and for the most part the gameplay is fun, engaging and very complex with a very high skill ceiling and always more to learn about every map, operator and how certain operators interact with the map to give you an edge, so for the most part the game is great!, but it definitely has some flaws, almost all of the flaws come from the parent company of the game, Ubisoft. the game is riddled with bugs, some of them minor like voice chat constantly turning on or off or just random noises playing throughout the entirety of the match even with the entire game muted, but there is also bugs that are totally game breaking, on certain maps like Oregon, you can clip into the ceiling, plant the diffuser and throw grenades at enemy below while they cant shoot you at all, and most of the bugs have the feeling that Ubisoft can fix every single bug in the game in under 24 hours but they for some reason chose not to, also Ubisoft's interface is buggy and conflicts with steam a lot, and trying too 100% the game is very tricky due to some achievements being completely bugged and seem to be unlocked at random so its very frustrating to somebody trying to complete the game.       Ubisoft sucks, don't recommend

First of all, fix your damn game, alright? You are a company that has enough money and enough workers to deliver a proper update. There are people out there, including me. I won’t say I didn’t make the choice myself or that I didn’t think about it, but still, a lot of people are paying money for this game. Me as an example: I bought the game instead of taking the free version. Why? Because I wanted to support this already rich company. I know that was my decision, but it still matters.

But you cannot, in any way, release a game with the resources you have and then push out an update that doesn’t even work. Multiple people have already complained that they hard crash when they start this new season. I’ve played this game on Xbox before, hundreds of hours. Now I’ve had it on PC for about half a year, and I really like it. I enjoy playing it with friends and alone.

But you cannot release updates like this when indie developers—sometimes just single people, probably under more stress than your massive company—are able to put out more stable updates than you, Ubisoft.


All in all, even though the pressure for this new season might be understandable, I still like the game. Try the free version first before deciding whether to spend money on it. I paid because I already knew what to expect from Ubisoft. If you’ve never had any connection with Ubisoft or never played Rainbow Six Siege, start with the free version. It’s free, so you can’t really go wrong.

Play the game, explore it, get used to it, put in maybe 5 to 10 hours, and then decide if you want to spend money on it. I would recommend it because it is fun. It can be fun playing with friends, even against sometimes broken AI that feels like it has wall hacks, which can actually be pretty funny and lead to good laughs.

It is a bit different from other shooters. In casual lobbies, you might end up with people screaming at each other, which can also be entertaining in its own way. You’ll probably have moments where you laugh a lot, and moments where you get frustrated.

So I can’t just say “get the game” without context, but I would say: try it for free first, and then decide if you want to pay for it or not. It is a fun game overall.

Tom Clancy's Rainbow Six Siege is easily my favorite FPS game of all time by a long shot, mostly due to the complexity that each round may possibly present to you. This is not your typical FPS especially when it comes to the grind that is the  Ranked playlist. Absolute singular focus is required by this game if you want to have even a hope of ranking up and if you have even the most miniscule of a distraction, your mental is immediately chalked. This is the only game that I know of as well, that not only requires the most absolute mental focus and game IQ/sense, while also having a massive player base largely comprised of individuals who, unfortunately for them, only have communal access to a total of 4 brain cells. The likely hood of one of these individuals to be on your team if you where to solo queue or maybe duo queue with your homie is higher an odd that of catching an STD by cracking a hooker that makes there residency of employ on the strip of Las Vegas. This is also true for that of the cheaters (aka lobotomites) that run rampant on the servers of Siege.

do not play this game if you want to enjoy any aspect of your life. This game will make you work with some of the stupidest people in the world who will only blame you when you lose. This game only provokes hatred and rage, but not a single dose of happiness. If you have not already come in contact with this game, save yourself.

It's quite hard to leave a bad review on a game you have played for over 2k hours over the span of almost a decade.

I do have things I do not like about R6 but they come in majority from design decisions from devs I do not agree with, specifically with nerfs regarding certain operators, changes who were completely unwarranted.

I understand that the purpose of these changes is to make the game more simple to grasp by newer players, pleasing a wider audience. Something that Tekken 8 tried to implement and failed miserably.

This is why I am leaving a positive review on this game and why I insist that you add this game to your library regardless of your fondness for the FPS genre. The balancing team has not only balanced the game, but also has managed to balance the rapport between newer players and seasoned veterans without destroying the identity of the game.

This is truly an impressive achievement.

Above all R6 is fun and very rewarding if you know what you are doing.
Playing this game will improve your:

- reaction time
- patience
- spacial awareness
- sense of timing
- strategic skills

Do not miss this opportunity to have fun and improve your skills playing one of the most unique, original, and innovative FPS games.

Ubisoft has fumbled a lot of things, but not R6!!

“Tactical brilliance, high tension, and one of the most unforgiving skill curves in FPS gaming.”

Tom Clancy's Rainbow Six Siege is not your typical shooter. It’s slow, methodical, and extremely punishing—where sound matters as much as aim, and one wrong peek can decide the entire round.

The core gameplay is built around tension. Attackers and defenders play completely different roles, and every round feels like a high-stakes puzzle. Drones, gadgets, destructible environments, and operator abilities all combine into a system that rewards planning and communication over raw reflexes.

The destruction system is still one of the best in any FPS. Walls, floors, and ceilings can all be breached, creating endless angles and forcing players to constantly adapt. No two rounds ever feel exactly the same.

Operator variety is another major strength. Each character brings unique tools that shape team strategy, and learning how they interact adds a lot of long-term depth to the game.

However, the learning curve is extremely steep. New players can feel overwhelmed quickly, and the community can be unforgiving. Smurfs, toxicity, and a lack of proper onboarding have always been issues. Solo queue experience can also be inconsistent depending on teammates.

Pros:

Deep tactical gameplay with high skill ceiling
Excellent destructible environments
Huge operator variety and team synergy
Extremely rewarding when mastered

Cons:

Very steep learning curve
Toxic or inconsistent matchmaking experience
Hard for new players to get into

Verdict:
If you’re willing to invest time and learn its systems, Rainbow Six Siege delivers one of the most intense and rewarding FPS experiences out there.

Recommended 👍

🧱 🪓 Tom Clancy's Rainbow Six Siege – Originally released back in 2015, this tactical, wall-shattering shooter is one of the most resilient, high-stakes games on the market. It is an absolute masterpiece of competitive tension—but let’s be brutally honest: this game is currently breathing, surviving, and thriving solely because of one single man: Jynxzi.

The Jynxzi Carrying Effect
It is an undisputed fact that the entire modern player base, pop-culture relevancy, and hype surrounding Rainbow Six Siege is being carried completely on the back of Jynxzi. Before he exploded into the king of Twitch and content creation, Siege was slowly coasting into a quiet, forgotten corner of the tactical shooter market.

His unhinged energy, tournament streams, and clip culture single-handedly injected millions of new and returning casual players back into the servers. Jynxzi did more for this game's marketing and active player count than a multi-million-dollar corporate campaign ever could. He is the literal life support machine keeping the lights on in this franchise.

The High-Rank Hellscape: Emerald and Diamond Realities
When you look at the core mechanics, the game is absolutely amazing. The adrenaline rush of pulling off a 1v3 clutch, sound-whoring an enemy through a bullet hole in a soft wall, or perfectly executing a hard-breach strategy is completely unmatched in the FPS world. The tactical depth is practically infinite.

... Unless, of course, you are completely hard-stuck Emerald or Diamond. If you hit this specific tier of MMR, the game transitions from a fun, tactical masterpiece into an absolute toxic, psychological nightmare. High-tier ranked matches are completely infested with cheating, wall-hacking losers on PC, and anti-recoil XIM/Cronus cheaters on consoles who use a mouse and keyboard to obliterate legitimate controller players. The sweat levels are so high and the community ego is so fragile that if you don't play perfectly with a coordinated 5-stack, you are guaranteed to tank your rank and lose your sanity.

Great Devs vs. The Trash Corporate Machine
The ground-level developer team actually deserves an immense amount of credit. Year after year, they continue to push out fantastic seasonal updates, rework classic maps to balance them for modern metas, and introduce innovative operator gadgets that prevent the gameplay from growing stale. They genuinely try to patch the holes in the ship and keep the competitive scene healthy.

The Ubisoft Corporate Tax:
While the actual developers are great, Ubisoft as a corporate entity is completely trash. The suit-and-tie executives continuously poison the well with predatory battle passes, overpriced monthly membership subscriptions, lazy cosmetic marketplace monetization, and an anti-cheat infrastructure that feels like it's held together by duct tape and prayers. The devs build a beautiful tactical machine, but Ubisoft corporate keeps slashing the tires for a quick buck.

🟢 Brilliant, unparalleled tactical gunplay and fully destructible environments that reward real brainpower over raw aim
📣 An incredible community resurgence and meme culture fueled entirely by the streaming phenomenon of Jynxzi
🛠️ Fantastic, consistent seasonal content and operator reworks from a dedicated ground-level dev team
🔴 A completely toxic, miserable experience if you are hard-stuck in Emerald or Diamond due to rampant cheaters and XIM users
🔴 Predictably greedy monetization and terrible corporate subscription decisions from upper management at Ubisoft

8/10 – "A flawless, high-octane tactical masterpiece at its core, but it's a game that is currently running on pure Jynxzi fuel while constantly dodging corporate sabotage from its own publisher."

I feel like the main issue this game has comes from the community itself, be it toxic teammates or cheaters. If u turn off voice chat and play casually w some friends u can still have fun in spite of these issues. 
There is a lot of variety in characters and strategies that u can use, but the ranked map pool can feel quite small after a while, esp if you end up playing the same map back to back. The new casino map is prt cool as well but it can get boring playing it in most of ur matches.
Over all a good game, difficult to learn as a beginner, u will lose a lot, but if u take the time to learn from ur mistakes, it can be very satisfying when u finally get the hang of it.

👍 Recommended

I bought this game to experience tactical teamwork and strategic gameplay.

Instead, I learned:

* How many different ways a teammate can accidentally flash me.
* That a 3-inch hole in a wall is somehow more dangerous than a nuclear warhead.
* The sound of my own footsteps is apparently louder than a marching band.
* Every enemy player is legally required to have a PhD in geometry and peek angles.

My first match:
“Guys, where should I reinforce?”

My team:
“YES.”

500 hours later, I still get jumpscared by random barricades exploding and still can’t tell if the footsteps are above me, below me, behind me, or in another country.

10/10 game.
I hate it.
See you tomorrow.

The premise of the game is a 10/10. Nothing short. Just a straight up 10/10. There's really no other game like it. It's kind of in a genre of it's own. The gameplay is fluid, the operators are creatively designed, the maps are competitive, etc.

What ruins Rainbow 6 Siege is Ubisoft... 

Being a fan of this game is nothing short of pure torture. It's like watching a child of yours slowly become frail from cancer. The best you can do is stay by their side and hope their condition improves, but since you're not the doctor you feel powerless. I wouldn't wish it on my worst enemy. Ubisoft constantly refuses to listen to their fans about their desires for the game. (I can't really blame the devs if they're underfunded/staffed. That's the company's fault.) They've been better about this over the last ~6 months, but the bar of expectations has been in hell for the better part of a decade. What more do you expect from a soulless corporation who's interests are controlled by equally as soulless shareholders?

Having said that, will I stop playing the game? 
Probably not. 

It's one of the best competitive fps games on the market and there's honestly a reason why. It's a very engaging and complex game that has a really high skill ceiling and is very satisfying if you put in the time to learn the mechanics, maps, and operators. If you enjoy having a decent quality of life and mental health though, stay FAR away from this game. If you want a good fps, give it a try I guess. Be sure to purchase the microtransactions, Ubisoft investors need more yachts!

Very fun game in moderate dosages. Yes you will probably want to scream and break your monitor after playing for more than 30m but regardless it is still fun. The majority of the maps are well done and fun to play on. The wide array of operators, abilties and guns keeps things fresh and makes each game feel slighly different. The whole battle pass/cosmtic system is kinda annoying but the base game is free and most others games like it also have it so its not that bad. I do wish they would release more new maps more often especially since the new casino map that just came out is really fun. They still should playtest maps/operators more often before releasing them as the Dokkaebi rework is horrible making 1vX fights unwinnable if the other team has her. I agree she was very annoying before and needed a rework but this rework does not make things any better in fact it might have made things worse than before. I also think shield operators need a balance pass in general (slight nerf in my opinion) but that is partially getting fixed next season with the new operator that is a counter to shields.
this game is a very action packed game with lots of strategies and ways to win
